steps: Go to https://developer.waveapps.com and read the OAuth guide
Navigate to https://developer.waveapps.com/hc/en-us/requests/new to submit an application request for API access
Once approved, log in to your Wave account at https://waveapps.com
Go to your Wave developer settings to register an OAuth application and obtain your Client ID and Client Secret
Set your OAuth redirect URI to match your application's callback URL
Use the authorization URL https://api.waveapps.com/oauth2/authorize/ to initiate the OAuth flow
Exchange the authorization code for an access token at https://api.waveapps.com/oauth2/token/
Send GraphQL queries to https://gql.waveapps.com/graphql/public with the access token

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: Your Wave OAuth application Client ID

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: Your Wave OAuth application Client Secret

notes: Wave accounting is free for core features; API access may require a Pro or Wave Advisor subscription
Users can only grant access to businesses on an active Pro or Wave Advisor plan
The Wave API uses GraphQL — all operations are POST requests to /graphql/public
OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow is required; no personal access tokens available

test_endpoint.description: Send a GraphQL query (e.g., { user { id firstName lastName } }) to verify credentials
